channel.title: Hapi — Happy News by Topic
channel.description: Uplifting stories from around the world, organised by topic.

item.title: Africa Forward Summit Unlocks $27 Billion for Clean Energy and Innovation
item.description: At the Nairobi Africa Forward Summit, 40 companies committed $31.5 billion across 30 projects spanning clean energy, agriculture, and AI — including $2 billion in renewable power for Rwanda alone. The pledges promise 600,000 new jobs across the continent, signalling surging global confidence in Africa's economic and technological future.

item.title: Chile's Lithium Exports Surge 175% as Its Clean Energy Role Accelerates
item.description: Chile posted $9.7 billion in exports in April 2026 — its 19th consecutive month of year-on-year trade growth — with lithium carbonate exports surging 175%, driven by the SQM-Codelco state partnership ramping up production. The figures cement Chile's role as a cornerstone of the global clean energy supply chain.

item.title: US April Jobs Report Shows Wages Up 3.6% and Strong Hiring Momentum
item.description: US nonfarm payrolls added 115,000 jobs in April while average hourly earnings rose 3.6% year-over-year, beating analyst estimates and signalling continued labour market resilience. The positive data offers reassurance to workers and businesses heading into the summer season.

item.title: Mayo Clinic Scientists Crack the Code on 'Zombie Cells' That Drive Aging
item.description: Researchers at Mayo Clinic developed synthetic DNA aptamers that selectively identify senescent 'zombie cells' linked to Alzheimer's, arthritis, and cancer — without harming healthy tissue. The breakthrough could unlock precision drug delivery to clear these cells from the body, potentially transforming treatment for over a billion people living with age-related diseases.

item.title: EU-Funded Project Helps People With Complete Spinal Cord Injuries Walk Again
item.description: Europe's ReverseParalysis project used next-generation brain-spine interfaces and adaptive machine learning to help two people with complete spinal cord injuries stand and walk, and two others regain use of their hands. The breakthrough, led by ONWARD Medical in the Netherlands, marks a major step toward restoring movement after previously permanent paralysis.

item.title: Scientists Engineer Exotic Quantum States With Extraordinary Error-Resistance
item.description: Researchers produced new forms of quantum matter using precisely timed magnetic pulses, creating states with exceptional resistance to the errors that typically derail quantum computers. The breakthrough could dramatically accelerate the path to stable quantum computing — with vast potential across medicine, materials science, and cryptography.

item.title: World Hits Record 692 GW of New Renewables in 2025 — Nearly Half of All Power Capacity
item.description: The International Renewable Energy Agency reports that renewables now represent nearly half of all global installed electricity capacity, after the world added a record 692 GW in 2025 — with solar alone contributing 511 GW, the largest single-technology annual energy addition in history. The milestone marks a pivotal turning point in the global energy transition.

item.title: China's Electric Trucks Hit 20% Market Share in Historic Clean Freight Shift
item.description: Electric trucks in China outsold diesel for the first time in late 2025 and maintained 45% year-on-year growth into 2026, representing one of the fastest clean-freight transitions in history. The shift signals a dramatic reduction in transport emissions across the world's largest commercial vehicle market.

item.title: Brazil Leads Launch of $125 Billion Fund That Pays Nations to Protect Their Forests
item.description: International partners led by Brazil are establishing the Tropical Forests Forever Facility — a $125 billion investment fund whose returns directly reward tropical nations that protect their forests. The scheme gives developing countries financial self-determination over conservation and local communities a direct stake in keeping their forests standing.

item.title: NHS Gene Therapy Gives Six-Year-Old the Gift of Seeing in the Dark
item.description: Saffie Sandford, born with a rare inherited blindness, can now see her parents' faces in the dark after receiving gene therapy at Great Ormond Street Hospital. A new UK study confirms the treatment is most effective for young children, opening the door to restoring sight for thousands born with this condition.

item.title: Vietnam Gives All 100 Million Citizens Free Annual Health Checkups Starting 2026
item.description: Every Vietnamese citizen is now entitled to at least one free annual medical checkup, backed by digital health records integrated into the national ID platform. The landmark policy marks a historic shift from treatment-focused to prevention-focused healthcare for a nation of 100 million people.

item.title: New At-Home HPV Test Detects High-Risk Cancer Strains With 95% Accuracy
item.description: The FDA-authorised Teal Wand lets women screen for 14 high-risk HPV strains at home with 95% accuracy compared to clinician-collected tests. The device dramatically lowers barriers to early cervical cancer detection, putting life-saving screening within reach of women who lack easy access to healthcare providers.

item.title: South Korean Activist Wins Goldman Prize for Asia's First Climate Court Victory
item.description: Borim Kim led a four-year legal battle that produced Asia's first-ever constitutional climate court ruling, forcing the South Korean government to set legally binding emissions reduction targets through 2049. The landmark victory is already inspiring follow-on climate cases in Japan and Taiwan, spreading the legal movement across the continent.

item.title: Niue Makes Pacific History Electing a Record Seven Women to Parliament
item.description: The Pacific island of Niue elected seven women to its 20-seat parliament — 35% female representation — surpassing the UN's 'critical mass' threshold of 30% and making Niue second only to New Zealand for women's parliamentary representation across the Pacific. The historic result marks a generational shift in leadership for the tiny island nation.

item.title: NEA Honors 2026 National Heritage Fellows — Masters of America's Living Folk Arts
item.description: The National Endowment for the Arts announced its 2026 National Heritage Fellows — the nation's highest folk arts honour — celebrating artists including Hawaiian weavers reviving a near-extinct basket-making tradition and Mexican son musicians sustaining regional heritage. Each fellow receives a $25,000 award in recognition of their contribution to living cultural traditions.

item.title: 'Michael' Jackson Biopic Sets the All-Time Opening Weekend Record for a Biopic
item.description: The Michael Jackson biopic opened to $97 million domestically and $217 million worldwide in its first weekend — the biggest debut in biopic history — drawing fans across generations to celebrate his artistry. The film's record-breaking success proves the enduring global appetite for music-driven cinema and storytelling.

item.title: Frieze New York's 15th Edition Brings 68 Galleries From 26 Countries to The Shed
item.description: Frieze New York's milestone 15th edition gathered 68 galleries from 26 countries at The Shed in Hudson Yards, with a special spotlight on Central and South American artists. The fair reinforced New York's role as a truly global hub for contemporary art and cross-cultural creative exchange.

item.title: Inter Miami Opens Nu Stadium — 25,000 Seats and Miami's Largest Urban Green Space
item.description: Inter Miami's new Nu Stadium at Miami Freedom Park opened to fans this spring, offering 25,000 seats and access to Miami's largest urban green space — over 100 acres of parks and gardens surrounding the venue. The landmark development represents a new model for community-integrated sports infrastructure in the United States.

item.title: Chester's Historic Leisure Centre Reopens With Expanded Wellness Spaces After £12M Makeover
item.description: The Brio Northgate Leisure Centre in Chester has reopened after a £12 million refurbishment featuring an expanded gym, new fitness studios, sauna, steamroom, and energy-saving solar panels. The revamped centre makes inclusive wellness facilities accessible to the wider community once again, with a greener footprint than before.

item.title: Nigerian Scientist Wins 'Green Nobel' for Saving Endangered Bats and Rainforest
item.description: Dr. Iroro Tanshi rediscovered a species of endangered roundleaf bat after a 50-year absence, then built a community-led wildfire brigade that stopped over 70 fire outbreaks protecting Nigeria's last remaining rainforest. Her extraordinary conservation work earned her the 2026 Goldman Environmental Prize — the world's highest grassroots environmental honour.

item.title: 30,000 Coho Salmon Return to California's Russian River — Double the Previous Record
item.description: More than 30,000 adult coho salmon migrated to the Russian River in 2024–2025 — double the previous season's record — following decades of habitat restoration, captive breeding, and improved water management. NOAA celebrated the milestone on Endangered Species Day as proof that patient, community-led conservation work truly pays off.

item.title: India Overtakes Japan to Become World's Fourth Largest Economy
item.description: India's nominal GDP has reached approximately $4.19 trillion, marginally surpassing Japan to become the world's fourth largest economy — a landmark for a nation of 1.4 billion people. Analysts project India could overtake Germany within three years.

item.title: Australia and the EU Sign Historic Free Trade Deal After Decades of Talks
item.description: Australia and the European Union have concluded a landmark free trade agreement, making 98% of Australian exports duty-free into a market of 450 million people. The A$10 billion deal covers critical minerals, renewable energy, food, and services — cementing a new era of partnership.

item.title: Women's Elite Sports Revenue Set to Hit $3 Billion in 2026 — Up 25%
item.description: Global revenues in women's elite sports are on track to surpass $3 billion in 2026, a 25% jump on the previous year. Viewership, sponsorships, and fan engagement are all at record highs, marking a transformative turning point for gender equity in professional athletics worldwide.

item.title: Scientists Connect a Time Crystal to the Outside World for the First Time
item.description: Researchers at Aalto University have linked a time crystal — matter that "ticks" perpetually without energy input — to an external mechanical device, controlling its behaviour for the first time. The breakthrough opens doors to ultra-precise quantum sensors and new forms of quantum computer memory.

item.title: Quantum Breakthrough Could Revolutionise Computing and Teleportation
item.description: Scientists announced a major quantum physics milestone in May 2026 that could make quantum teleportation and computing far more practical. The research demonstrates a new approach to maintaining stable quantum states, tackling one of the field's most persistent engineering challenges.

item.title: Scientists Uncover 6-Million-Year-Old Ice in Antarctica — Oldest Ever Found
item.description: A US research team working in Antarctica's Allan Hills has directly dated ice at 6 million years old — the oldest ever confirmed. The ancient ice holds tiny sealed air bubbles containing Earth's prehistoric atmosphere, opening an extraordinary window into long-term climate history.

item.title: US Renewables Beat Natural Gas as Top Electricity Source for the First Time
item.description: In March 2026, wind, solar, hydropower, and biomass collectively generated 35% of US electricity — edging out natural gas at 34.4% for the first time in history. March also set a record for wind power output, and analysts say renewables could lead the grid again next spring.

item.title: EU Awards €1.09 Billion to Nine Green Hydrogen Projects Across Seven Nations
item.description: The European Commission selected nine hydrogen production projects under the third European Hydrogen Bank auction, awarding over €1 billion from the EU Innovation Fund. The projects will add 1.1 GW of electrolyser capacity and produce 1.3 million tonnes of renewable hydrogen over their first decade.

item.title: Solar Firm Plans $750 Million Power Grids Across Four African Nations
item.description: Renewvia Solar Africa is building $750 million in solar power grids across Uganda, Rwanda, Ethiopia, and DR Congo, targeting 2.1 million new electricity connections. The investment marks one of the continent's most ambitious distributed energy expansions.

item.title: WHO Confirms Malaria Vaccine Saves One in Eight Children's Lives
item.description: A landmark WHO study confirms the RTS,S malaria vaccine prevented 1 in 8 child deaths in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi during its first rollout years. Results are strong enough that scientists expect even better outcomes as 25 African nations now offer the vaccine.

item.title: Blood Test Predicts How IBD Patients Respond to Treatment Before They Start
item.description: Researchers at Imperial College London have developed a blood test that can predict a patient's disease trajectory and likely response to therapy before treatment begins. For people with inflammatory bowel disease, it could end years of painful trial-and-error and get patients on the right path from day one.

item.title: Ancient Chinese Exercise Rivals Brisk Walking for Lowering Blood Pressure
item.description: A major clinical trial found that baduanjin, an ancient Chinese movement practice, lowered blood pressure in adults with stage 1 hypertension just as effectively as a daily brisk walk. The finding offers a gentle, culturally accessible path to cardiovascular health for millions worldwide.

item.title: Three EU Countries Hit 90% HPV Vaccination Rate — a Milestone for Girls' Health
item.description: Three European Union nations have reached the EU's target of 90% HPV vaccination coverage among girls by age 15, putting them on a clear path toward eliminating cervical cancer as a public health threat. The wider EU rollout continues to accelerate with countries rapidly closing the gap.

item.title: Gen Z Homeowners Now Outpace Millennials at the Same Age
item.description: New data reveals Gen Z adults are buying homes at a faster rate than millennials did at the same stage of life — and more are doing it without parental help. The milestone signals surprising financial resilience in a generation that came of age amid economic upheaval.

item.title: Canon Oceania Marks 20 Years of Community Grants With $50,000 Funding Round
item.description: Canon Oceania has opened its 2026 grants program, celebrating two decades of supporting communities across Australia and New Zealand. Since 2007, the initiative has distributed $648,000 to 130 organisations across community, education, environment, and First Nations categories.

item.title: John Travolta Gets Surprise Honorary Palme d'Or at Cannes — Calls It "Beyond the Oscar"
item.description: Hollywood legend John Travolta was awarded a surprise honorary Palme d'Or at the 2026 Cannes Film Festival, prompting a visibly moved and joyful response from the star. The honour came alongside the premiere of his directorial debut, a heartfelt family film inspired by his own childhood love of aviation.

item.title: MayFest 2026 Celebrates Route 66 Centennial With Art and Music Across Tulsa
item.description: Tulsa's beloved MayFest festival is this year honouring 100 years of Route 66 with vibrant art installations, live music, and community events across the city. The outdoor celebration draws thousands of families and serves as a joyful tribute to America's most storied road.

item.title: Gold Gala 2026 Celebrates AAPI Excellence in Entertainment, Arts, and Culture
item.description: Gold House's Gold Gala 2026 brought together leading Asian American and Pacific Islander creators, artists, and executives for a night of celebration and recognition. The event highlights the growing influence of AAPI voices across entertainment, business, and American cultural life.

item.title: Brewers' Misiorowski Sets Statcast-Era Fastball Record, Topping 100 mph
item.description: Milwaukee Brewers pitcher Jacob Misiorowski made Statcast history by clocking his fastball at over 100 mph in a victory over the New York Yankees on May 15. The 24-year-old All-Star, already posting an outstanding ERA this season, is establishing himself as one of baseball's most electrifying young arms.

item.title: New Zealand Archers Dominate at 2026 World Archery Oceania Championships
item.description: New Zealand delivered a commanding performance on home soil at the 2026 World Archery Oceania Target Championships in Auckland, topping the medal standings across individual and team events. The result is a proud moment for New Zealand's flourishing archery community.

item.title: Women's College Sports Reach Record US Participation With Rising Leadership
item.description: Women's sports have reached record NCAA participation levels in 2026, with women also gaining ground as coaches and athletic directors. The milestone reflects sustained momentum toward gender equity in collegiate athletics, driven by expanded programmes and rising student demand.

item.title: North Atlantic Right Whales Welcome 23 Calves — Highest Count Since 2009
item.description: The 2026 North Atlantic right whale calving season concluded with 23 new calves spotted, the highest count in 15 years. Thirteen returning mothers calved sooner than the recent average, suggesting improving reproductive health in a critically endangered population of around 384 animals.

item.title: California's Gray Wolves Grow Into 10 Roaming Packs After Decades of Rewilding
item.description: Gray wolves, absent from California for decades, now roam the state in an estimated 10 separate packs totalling 50–70 animals. Sustained conservation efforts and the species' own resilience have driven a recovery that many once thought impossible for the Golden State.

item.title: America's Bald Eagle Soars Back to 14,000 Breeding Pairs From Near Extinction
item.description: Once reduced to fewer than 500 breeding pairs, the bald eagle has rebounded to around 14,000 pairs across the United States — a testament to the Endangered Species Act and the banning of DDT. Celebrated on Endangered Species Day, it stands as one of conservation's greatest modern victories.

item.title: Garden River First Nation Becomes 50% Co-Owner of Ontario's Largest Solar Farm
item.description: Garden River First Nation has secured an equal equity stake in Ontario's 253-megawatt planned solar farm — a billion project expected to generate million in annual revenue for the community. Chief Karen Bell called it a landmark step for Indigenous economic sovereignty in Canada's clean energy future.

item.title: Brazil Approves Critical Minerals Strategy With Billion in Tax Incentives
item.description: Brazil's Chamber of Deputies passed the National Policy for Critical Minerals 343-97, unlocking a billion guarantor fund and billion in tax credits to transform the country's vast lithium, nickel, and rare-earth deposits into a pillar of the global clean energy transition.

item.title: Ethiopia Breaks Ground on Africa's Largest Airport, a .5 Billion Vision
item.description: Led by Ethiopian Airlines and designed by Zaha Hadid Architects, construction has begun on the Bishoftu International Airport — a solar-powered, 110-million-passenger hub poised to make Ethiopia the gateway to the world for the entire continent. Phase I is set to open in 2030.

item.title: Japan Scientists Crack the Code for Instantly Detecting Quantum W States
item.description: Researchers in Japan have built a stable photonic circuit that instantly identifies elusive quantum W states — configurations critical for teleportation and ultra-secure communication. The system runs without manual adjustment, clearing a major bottleneck on the road to practical quantum networks.

item.title: China Achieves a Perfect 10th Space Station Supply Mission With Tianzhou-10
item.description: Tianzhou-10 docked flawlessly with China's Tiangong space station on May 11, delivering over six tons of supplies including new extravehicular spacesuits and six scientific payloads. The milestone marks ten consecutive successful Tianzhou missions — the Chinese ideal of shiquan shimei, perfection in ten.

item.title: Quantum Encryption Successfully Sent Across 120 Kilometres of Optical Fibre
item.description: An international research team has demonstrated stable quantum key distribution over 120 km of standard fibre using semiconductor quantum dots — maintaining ultra-low error rates continuously for six hours without manual tuning. The result brings truly unhackable, real-world quantum communication a decisive step closer.

item.title: Cuba Is Pulling Off One of the Fastest Solar Revolutions on Earth
item.description: Cuba has tripled its renewable electricity capacity to over 20% in just one year, with 49 solar parks now feeding the national grid and Chinese investment accelerating dozens more. Targeting 100% renewables by 2050, the island's green leap offers an inspiring example of rapid solar transformation.

item.title: Colombia Already Surpasses Its Marine 30x30 Conservation Goal
item.description: Colombia has protected 47% of its marine territory — far exceeding the global 30x30 target — while conserving 26% of its land and expanding its bio-economy to create half a million jobs. The country's record offers a powerful proof of concept for biodiversity-led, job-rich sustainable development.

item.title: Australia's Solar Generation Breaks Q1 Records as Renewables Hit 46.5%
item.description: Australia set an all-time high of 2,706 MW in grid-scale solar generation during Q1 2026, while renewables supplied 46.5% of electricity — the highest first-quarter share ever. Rooftop solar also hit a record 4,090 MW, putting Australia ahead of schedule to beat its 2030 targets.

item.title: Africa Saves 20 Million Lives From Measles Through Rising Vaccination
item.description: A landmark WHO and Gavi report confirms that expanding immunisation programmes across Africa have averted nearly 20 million measles deaths since 2000, halving mortality on the continent. Over 500 million children were protected through routine campaigns, and three island nations achieved full measles elimination.

item.title: NHS Rolls Out a One-Minute Cancer Immunotherapy Jab for 14,000 Patients
item.description: England's NHS has begun offering pembrolizumab — a powerful immunotherapy for 14 cancer types — as a 60-second injection instead of a two-hour intravenous infusion. The shift saves over 100,000 NHS staff hours annually and gives patients back hours of their lives each treatment cycle.

item.title: Western Africa Pledges Health Investment Roadmap Linking Doctors to Economic Growth
item.description: Health and finance ministers from 12 Western and Central African nations met in Accra to launch the World Bank's 'Fit to Prosper' strategy, pledging to tie health infrastructure spending directly to job creation and economic development. The plan charts a new model where healthy populations and thriving economies build each other up.

item.title: Vietnam Removes Park Fences, Opening Hanoi's Green Spaces to Everyone
item.description: Hanoi has taken down the iron fences surrounding its major parks, letting residents freely stroll, exercise, and gather on the grass. The city is also building six new parks as part of a compact-green urban model that prioritises public space, water features, and tree canopy for all.

item.title: England's Renters' Rights Act Gives 11 Million Tenants Historic New Powers
item.description: England's landmark Renters' Rights Act has come into force, abolishing no-fault evictions and giving 11 million renters the strongest tenancy protections in a generation. Tenants can now challenge unfair rent increases and enjoy greater security — the biggest shake-up of renting law in over 30 years.

item.title: Peru Welcomes Record 823,000 Visitors in Q1 as South American Tourism Booms
item.description: Peru's tourism sector welcomed 823,863 international visitors in Q1 2026 — up 3.5% year-on-year — with Chile, the US, Ecuador, and Bolivia among the leading sources. The surge reflects growing cross-border travel enthusiasm and deepening cultural connections across the South American neighbourhood.

item.title: V&A East Opens a Joyful New Cultural Home in London's Olympic Park
item.description: A decade in the making, the V&A East Museum welcomed visitors to Stratford with free permanent galleries spanning global art, fashion, architecture, and design. Its inaugural exhibition explores 125 years of Black music-making in Britain — a vibrant start for the East Bank cultural quarter.

item.title: Vin Diesel's Emotional Paul Walker Tribute Moves Cannes to Its Feet
item.description: At the Cannes Film Festival's 25th anniversary screening of The Fast and the Furious, Vin Diesel broke down honouring his late co-star Paul Walker, moving audiences worldwide. The moment captured the unique power of cinema to sustain friendship, love, and memory long after the credits roll.

item.title: KATSEYE Announces Landmark Arena Tour Spanning UK, Europe and North America
item.description: Pop group KATSEYE — the K-pop-meets-global-pop ensemble — has announced a major 2026 arena tour across the UK, Europe, and North America, marking a cultural milestone for a group that has joyfully bridged Asian and Western music scenes. The tour reflects a new era of genre-crossing, cross-continental pop.

item.title: Toronto Tempo Makes History as WNBA's First-Ever Team Outside the USA
item.description: Canada's Toronto Tempo took the court on May 8 before a sold-out Coca-Cola Coliseum crowd, marking the WNBA's first international expansion in the league's history. The arrival of women's professional basketball in Canada opens a new chapter for the sport across North America.

item.title: 18-Year-Old Gout Gout Runs Faster Than Usain Bolt Did at the Same Age
item.description: Australian sprinter Gout Gout clocked a blazing 19.67 seconds in the 200m — breaking Usain Bolt's under-20 world record by 0.26 seconds and becoming the first Australian to go sub-20. At 18, the South Sudanese-born athlete is the brightest new star in global sprinting, with Bolt himself offering warm encouragement.

item.title: Wu Yize Wins World Snooker Championship in a Breathtaking 18-17 Final
item.description: China's Wu Yize captured the 2026 World Snooker Championship at Sheffield's Crucible Theatre, edging Shaun Murphy 18-17 in one of the most dramatic finals in recent memory. The victory marks a thrilling new chapter for snooker and a landmark achievement for the young Chinese champion.

item.title: New Zealand's Kākāpō Breaks All Records With Over 100 Chicks in One Season
item.description: The critically endangered kākāpō — the world's heaviest and only flightless parrot — has hatched over 100 chicks in 2026, its best breeding season ever and a near-doubling of the wild population. Decades of work by DOC rangers and Ngāi Tahu have turned what seemed impossible into a conservation triumph.

item.title: Mountain Gorilla Census Documents Hopeful Recovery in Uganda's Bwindi Forest
item.description: Scientists in Uganda's Bwindi Impenetrable National Park are conducting a meticulous gorilla headcount using camera traps and field photography. The census captures a population slowly climbing back from the brink — a tribute to decades of dedicated international conservation effort.

item.title: Humpback Whale Recovery Is So Strong It's Changing Who Fathers the Calves
item.description: A landmark study finds that humpback whale populations have rebounded so powerfully that the social dynamics of which males father calves are measurably shifting — a sign of a thriving, complex wild community. Eastern Australia's humpback population has returned to pre-whaling levels, growing at 10-11% annually.
